content.claim: Poland's 2017 reversal of the gimnazjum reform (returning to eight-year primary schools) was explicitly political and populist in rationale, disregarding positive PISA outcomes and labour-market evidence supporting the 1999 reform.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Characterization of 'populist' is an analytical judgment rather than neutral description
Does not quantify voter preference heterogeneity or distinguish between genuine public concern and elite framing
Success evidence (PISA rankings, labour outcomes) is attributable to the reform but may not capture all relevant distributional or social costs
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
